content.problemStatement:
You have an undirected complete graph with NNN nodes, numbered from 111 to NNN.

The Value of a simple path in this graph, say (u1,u2,…,uk)(u_1, u_2, \ldots, u_k)(u1​,u2​,…,uk​), is the Bitwise XOR of all the node numbers. That is, the Value is equal to u1⊕u2⊕…⊕uku_1 \oplus u_2 \oplus \ldots \oplus u_ku1​⊕u2​⊕…⊕uk​.

You are given QQQ queries in the form of uuu and vvv. Chef asks you to find a simple path from node uuu to node vvv, such that, among all the simple paths from uuu to vvv, this path has the maximum Value. In case there are multiple simple paths with the same maximum Value, find any simple path from among those that has the minimum length.

**Note:**


A path in the graph is a sequence of nodes v1,v2,…,vkv_1, v_2, \ldots, v_kv1​,v2​,…,vk​ such that each pair of adjacent (consecutive) nodes in this sequence is connected by an edge.
The length of the path is the number of nodes in it.
A simple path is a path in which all nodes are distinct.
A complete graph is a graph where every pair of distinct vertices is connected by an edge.

Input Format

The first line of input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv.


Output Format

In the first line for each query print two integers, MaxValueMaxValueMaxValue, and kkk, denoting the maximum ValueValueValue achievable, and the number of nodes in the path.
In the next line print kkk space-separated integers, denoting the nodes of the simple path in order.
constraints: **Subtask 2 (27 points):** 2≤N≤105, where N=2m−1 for some integer m2 \leq N \leq 10^5, \text{ where } N = 2^m - 1 \text{ for some integer } m2≤N≤105, where N=2m−1 for some integer m

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first query the maximum Value you can achieve from node 111 to node 444 will be 777. The minimum length path will be 1→2→41 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 41→2→4 having Value of 1⊕2⊕4=71 \oplus 2 \oplus 4 = 71⊕2⊕4=7.
For the second query the maximum Value you can achieve from node 222 to node 555 will be 777. The minimum length path will be 2→52 \rightarrow 52→5 having Value of 2⊕5=72 \oplus 5 = 72⊕5=7.
